_meta.description: Single source of truth for UI structure. Documents current layout, target structure during refactors, and history. Agents MUST refer to this before any structural changes.

current_structure.description: Initial placeholder structure (Phase 1 complete, Phase 2 not started)

refactor_history.title: Initial Architecture & Rules Setup
refactor_history.description: Established architectural foundation, coding rules, and configuration guides
refactor_history.notes: No code changes, only documentation and rules

rules: Any structural change (new page, hiding old page, renaming route, removing component) MUST be documented here FIRST
Before making breaking changes, agent MUST ask user: 'I plan to [change]. Approve?'
If user says yes, agent updates 'target_structure' and documents in 'refactor_history'
If refactor spans multiple sessions, agent updates 'current_structure' to match actual state before resuming
At end of each session, verify manifest matches actual code structure
Never hide old pages without documenting: which old page is being hidden, where to find it in git history, what replaces it
on_session_resume: Agent MUST read this manifest first
If 'refactor_in_progress' is true, agent should resume at the exact state documented
If actual code structure doesn't match 'current_structure', agent should ask user to clarify which is source of truth
Agent should NOT proceed if manifest and code are out of sync
on_breaking_changes: Removing a page? Document: old path, new location (or that it's gone), why, when users will see change
Renaming a route? Document: old path, new path, redirect strategy
Hiding a component? Document: which component, where it was, what replaces it, when this becomes active
Merging two pages? Document: page A path, page B path, new merged path, data migration strategy
